description: Vibrant, vertical flowers that bring height and drama to borders and containers. Known for their 'dragon-mouth' shape.

characteristics.waterNeeds: Moderate — keep moist but not waterlogged

care.sowIndoors: Sow seeds thinly on surface of moist seed compost, do not cover as they need light to germinate. Keep at 18-21°C. Germination 7-14 days.
care.sowOutdoors: Sow thinly in prepared seedbed in sunny, well-drained spot.
care.hardenOff: Gradually acclimatize plants to outdoor conditions over 7-10 days before planting out.
care.plantOut: Plant out after all danger of frost has passed.
care.watering: Water regularly, especially during dry spells. Avoid overhead watering to reduce rust.
care.feeding: Feed with a high-potash liquid feed every 2-4 weeks once flowering begins.
care.harvest: Cut flower stems for indoor display when the first few florets are open.
care.overwintering: In milder areas, established plants may overwinter, especially if protected.

propagation.cuttingsMethod: Take 5-10cm non-flowering shoots in late summer, root in moist compost.

deadheading.notes: Crucial for continuous flowering. Pinch off spent flower spikes at the base.

seedSaving.method: Allow some flower spikes to go to seed at the end of the season. Collect the dry, papery seed pods once they start to split open.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Small, capsule-like pods that develop along the flower stalk.

funFact: The common name 'snapdragon' comes from the flower's resemblance to a dragon's snout, which snaps open and shut when squeezed.
